title: hulsea vestita subsp. pygmaea
intro: Hulsea vestita subsp. pygmaea, also known as dwarf hulsea, is a subspecies of the Hulsea vestita plant, a member of the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the western United States. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and as an antiseptic. Its health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe coughs, and aid in digestion. Hulsea vestita subsp. pygmaea can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile herbal remedy.
